hypothesis: BEAR entries when VIX >= 23 are structurally unprofitable in both IS and OOS. High-fear VIX (≥23) inflates put premium, causing -10% stop to fire on small adverse moves (C3/L149). Blocking these trades improves net P&L without removing any winning setups.
winner_rationale: VIX≥23: 0/3 SW hurt (cleanest), IS_delta=+$790 (best among RATIFY candidates), same OOS_delta=+$420. VIX≥22 had 1/3 SW hurt. VIX 22-23 IS bears (n=2) are mildly profitable (+$45) and correctly excluded.

G5_anchor_no_regression.detail: Anchor dates 4/29 5/1 5/4 all have VIX<23; no anchor trades removed

ratification_basis: OP-22 auto-ratify: OOS_positive AND WF≥0.70 AND sub_window_stable AND anchor_no_regression. J standing directive: 'I am no blocker. if its profitable implement it'
root_cause: VIX≥23 = high-fear environment. Put options carry inflated IV → premium is expensive relative to directional move size → -10% stop fires on small adverse swings (C3/L149 doctrine: SPY-price edge ≠ option edge at high IV). Bearish setups still pattern-fire correctly, but option mechanics kill the trade before it plays out.

implementation.live_activation_status: PARAMS_UPDATED — heartbeat.md activation pending (add filter: block bears if VIX >= vix_bear_hard_cap)
implementation.revert: set vix_bear_hard_cap to 0 or null in params.json; remove gate from heartbeat.md
